title: Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)
content:

{{C|Compact disc}} |release date=[[February 1]], [[1995]] {{C|Cassette}}{{WebCite|url=https://publicrecords.copyright.gov/detailed-record/voyager_12936173|text=Tales of the Jedi :full cast audio drama.|work=the [[Wikipedia:United States Copyright Office|U.S. Copyright Office Public Records System]]|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/LMh3h}} |rereleased=[[May 5]], [[2005]] {{C|Compact disc}} |media type=*Cassette tape{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Tales-Tom-Veitch/dp/1570421382|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi}} *Compact disc |run time=2 hours 45 minutes
(165 minutes) |discs=3 |cassettes=2 |pages= |isbn=9781570421389 |timeline=[[4000 BBY/Legends|4000]]–[[3999 BBY]]''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' |series=[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] |preceded by= |followed by=[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'']] }} '''''Tales of the Jedi''''' is an audio drama adaptation of [[Tom Veitch]]'s [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (miniseries)|''Tales of the Jedi: Knights of the Old Republic'']] and [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising|''Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising'']] comics. It was adapted by [[John Whitman]] and was first released on cassette tape by [[Time Warner AudioBooks]] in [[1995]]. Later on [[May 5]], [[2005]] it was released on compact disc by [[HighBridge Audio]]. ==Publisher's summary== '''''"For over a thousand generations, the Jedi Knights were the most powerful, most respected force in the galaxy. . . The guardians of peace and justice in the Old Republic." With those words, Obi-Wan Kenobi hinted at the grand legend, the era of the Jedi Knights.''''' Follow the adventures of Ulic Qel-Droma and Nomi Sunrider, young Jedi Knights drawn into battle against the dark side, a battle that may decide the fate of the planet and the galaxy! ==Plot summary== {{Plot|adaptation}} ==Continuity== The subplot with the raid against the [[Ithullan ore hauler]], the capture of [[Finhead Stonebone]], and mission to claim Nomi's [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] are not included in the story. Instead, these portions are replaced by some elements from [[Freedon Nadd Uprising|''The Freedon Nadd Uprising'']] such as the Naddists's terrorist acts on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] and the Ketos's trip to Coruscant. Eventually it is Gudb who is sent again against Nomi instead of Finhead. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|soundcat=Audio files from Tales of the Jedi (audio)}} ===Editions=== *{{ISBN|9781570421389}}; [[February 1]], [[1995]]; [[Time Warner AudioBooks]]; Cassette tape *{{ISBN|9781565119734}}; [[May 5]], [[2005]]; [[HighBridge Audio]]; Compact disc ===Cover gallery=== File:TotJCDCover.jpg|Audio
